text_original: Dui intimates that (under its conditions) there will be progress and attainment. (But) it will be advantageous to be firm and correct.
text_normalized: Dui intimates that (under its conditions) there will be progress and attainment. (But) it will be advantageous to be firm and correct.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=Judgment.

text_original: The first NINE, undivided, shows the pleasure of (inward) harmony. There will be good fortune.
text_normalized: The first NINE, undivided, shows the pleasure of (inward) harmony. There will be good fortune.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=first.

text_original: The second NINE, undivided, shows the pleasure arising from (inward) sincerity. There will be good fortune. Occasion for repentance will disappear.
text_normalized: The second NINE, undivided, shows the pleasure arising from (inward) sincerity. There will be good fortune. Occasion for repentance will disappear.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=second.

text_original: The third SIX, divided, shows its subject bringing round himself whatever can give pleasure. There will be evil.
text_normalized: The third SIX, divided, shows its subject bringing round himself whatever can give pleasure. There will be evil.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=third.

text_original: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject deliberating about what to seek his pleasure in, and not at rest. He borders on what would be injurious, but there will be cause for joy.
text_normalized: The fourth NINE, undivided, shows its subject deliberating about what to seek his pleasure in, and not at rest. He borders on what would be injurious, but there will be cause for joy.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fourth.

text_original: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject trusting in one who would injure him. The situation is perilous.
text_normalized: The fifth NINE, undivided, shows its subject trusting in one who would injure him. The situation is perilous.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fifth.

text_original: The topmost SIX, divided, shows the pleasure of its subject in leading and attracting others.
text_normalized: The topmost SIX, divided, shows the pleasure of its subject in leading and attracting others.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=top.
